coreClasses.name: Barbarian
coreClasses.description: A fierce warrior who can enter a battle rage.

classFeatures.name: Rage
classFeatures.description: A barbarian can call upon inner reserves of strength and ferocity, granting her additional combat prowess. At 1st level, a barbarian can rage for a number of rounds per day equal to 4 + her Constitution modifier. While in a rage, a barbarian gains a +2 bonus on melee attack rolls, melee damage rolls, thrown weapon damage rolls, and Will saving throws. She also takes a –2 penalty to Armor Class and gains 2 temporary hit points per Hit Die.

classFeatures.name: Fast Movement
classFeatures.description: The barbarian's land speed is faster than the norm for her race by +10 feet. This benefit applies only when she is wearing no armor, light armor, or medium armor, and not carrying a heavy load.

conditions: no armor, light armor, or medium armor

coreClasses.name: Bard
coreClasses.description: A master of song, speech, and the magic they contain.

classFeatures.name: Bardic Knowledge
classFeatures.description: A bard adds half his class level (minimum 1) to all Knowledge skill checks and may make all Knowledge skill checks untrained.

mechanics.skillBonus: half class level (min 1) to all Knowledge checks

classFeatures.name: Bardic Performance
classFeatures.description: A bard is trained to use the Perform skill to create magical effects on those around him. He can use this ability for a number of rounds per day equal to 4 + his Charisma modifier. At each level after 1st a bard can use bardic performance for 2 additional rounds per day. Starting a bardic performance is a standard action, but it can be maintained each round as a free action.

mechanics.actionType: standard action to start, free action to maintain

coreClasses.weaponProficiencies: Simple weapons, longsword, rapier, sap, short sword, shortbow, whip

coreClasses.name: Cleric
coreClasses.description: A devout follower of a deity who wields divine magic.

classFeatures.name: Aura
classFeatures.description: A cleric of a chaotic, evil, good, or lawful deity has a particularly powerful aura corresponding to the deity's alignment (see detect evil for details).

classFeatures.name: Channel Energy
classFeatures.description: Regardless of alignment, any cleric can release a wave of energy by channeling the power of her faith through her holy symbol. This energy can be used to cause or heal damage, depending on the type of energy channeled and the creatures targeted. A good cleric channels positive energy and can choose to deal damage to undead creatures or to heal living creatures. An evil cleric channels negative energy and can choose to deal damage to living creatures or to heal undead creatures.

classFeatures.name: Domains
classFeatures.description: A cleric's deity influences her alignment, what magic she can perform, her values, and how others see her. A cleric chooses two domains from among those belonging to her deity. Each domain grants a number of domain powers, dependent upon the level of the cleric, as well as a number of bonus spells.

coreClasses.name: Druid
coreClasses.description: A servant of nature who commands elemental forces and animal companions.

classFeatures.name: Nature Bond
classFeatures.description: At 1st level, a druid forms a bond with nature. This bond can take one of two forms. The first is a close tie to the natural world, granting the druid one of the following cleric domains: Air, Animal, Earth, Fire, Plant, Water, or Weather. The second option is to form a close bond with an animal companion.

choices: Domain (Air, Animal, Earth, Fire, Plant, Water, Weather)

classFeatures.name: Nature Sense
classFeatures.description: A druid gains a +2 bonus on Knowledge (nature) and Survival checks.

classFeatures.name: Wild Empathy
classFeatures.description: A druid can improve the initial attitude of an animal. This ability functions just like a Diplomacy check to improve the attitude of a person. The druid rolls 1d20 and adds her druid level and her Charisma modifier to determine the wild empathy check result.

coreClasses.weaponProficiencies: Clubs, daggers, darts, javelins, maces, quarterstaffs, scimitars, sickles, slings, spears
coreClasses.armorProficiencies: Light and medium non-metal armor, non-metal shields

coreClasses.name: Fighter
coreClasses.description: A master of martial combat skilled with a variety of weapons and armor.

classFeatures.name: Bonus Feats
classFeatures.description: At 1st level, and at every even level thereafter, a fighter gains a bonus feat in addition to those gained from normal advancement. These bonus feats must be selected from those listed as Combat Feats.

coreClasses.name: Monk
coreClasses.description: A master of martial arts, harnessing inner power.

classFeatures.name: AC Bonus
classFeatures.description: When unarmored and unencumbered, the monk adds her Wisdom bonus (if any) to her AC and her CMD. In addition, a monk gains a +1 bonus to AC and CMD at 4th level. This bonus increases by 1 for every four monk levels thereafter, up to a maximum of +5 at 20th level.

classFeatures.name: Flurry of Blows
classFeatures.description: Starting at 1st level, a monk can make a flurry of blows as a full-attack action. When doing so, he may make one additional attack, taking a –2 penalty on all of his attack rolls, as if using the Two-Weapon Fighting feat. These attacks can be any combination of unarmed strikes and attacks with a monk special weapon.

classFeatures.name: Stunning Fist
classFeatures.description: At 1st level, the monk gains Stunning Fist as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites. At 4th level, and every 4 levels thereafter, the monk gains the ability to apply a new condition to the target of his Stunning Fist.

classFeatures.name: Unarmed Strike
classFeatures.description: At 1st level, a monk gains Improved Unarmed Strike as a bonus feat. A monk's attacks may be with fist, elbows, knees, and feet. This means that a monk may make unarmed strikes with his hands full. A monk's unarmed strikes deal lethal damage.

coreClasses.weaponProficiencies: Club, crossbow (light or heavy), dagger, handaxe, javelin, kama, nunchaku, quarterstaff, sai, shortspear, short sword, shuriken, siangham, sling

coreClasses.name: Paladin
coreClasses.description: A holy champion who smites evil with divine power.

classFeatures.name: Aura of Good
classFeatures.description: The power of a paladin's aura of good (see the detect good spell) is equal to her paladin level.

classFeatures.name: Detect Evil
classFeatures.description: At will, a paladin can use detect evil, as the spell. A paladin can, as a move action, concentrate on a single item or individual within 60 feet and determine if it is evil, learning the strength of its aura as if having studied it for 3 rounds.

classFeatures.name: Smite Evil
classFeatures.description: Once per day, a paladin can call out to the powers of good to aid her in her struggle against evil. As a swift action, the paladin chooses one target within sight to smite. If this target is evil, the paladin adds her Charisma bonus (if any) to her attack rolls and adds her paladin level to all damage rolls made against the target of her smite.

coreClasses.name: Ranger
coreClasses.description: A skilled hunter and tracker who dwells on the edges of civilization.

classFeatures.name: 1st Favored Enemy
classFeatures.description: At 1st level, a ranger selects a creature type from the ranger favored enemies table. He gains a +2 bonus on Bluff, Knowledge, Perception, Sense Motive, and Survival checks against creatures of his selected type. Likewise, he gets a +2 bonus on weapon attack and damage rolls against them.

mechanics.skillBonus: +2 to Bluff, Knowledge, Perception, Sense Motive, Survival vs favored enemy
mechanics.combatBonus: +2 attack and damage vs favored enemy

classFeatures.name: Track
classFeatures.description: A ranger adds half his level (minimum 1) to Survival skill checks made to follow or identify tracks.

mechanics.skillBonus: half ranger level (min 1) to Survival for tracking

classFeatures.name: Wild Empathy
classFeatures.description: A ranger can improve the initial attitude of an animal. This ability functions just like a Diplomacy check to improve the attitude of a person. The ranger rolls 1d20 and adds his ranger level and his Charisma modifier to determine the wild empathy check result.

coreClasses.name: Rogue
coreClasses.description: A scoundrel who uses stealth and trickery to overcome obstacles.

classFeatures.name: Sneak Attack
classFeatures.description: If a rogue can catch an opponent when he is unable to defend himself effectively from her attack, she can strike a vital spot for extra damage. This extra damage is 1d6 at 1st level.

classFeatures.name: Trapfinding
classFeatures.description: A rogue adds 1/2 her level to Perception skill checks made to locate traps and to Disable Device checks (minimum 1). She can use Disable Device to disarm magical traps.

mechanics.skillBonus: half level to Perception vs traps and Disable Device

coreClasses.weaponProficiencies: Simple weapons, hand crossbow, rapier, sap, shortbow, short sword

coreClasses.name: Sorcerer
coreClasses.description: A spellcaster who draws on inherent magic from a draconic or other exotic bloodline.

classFeatures.name: Bloodline
classFeatures.description: Each sorcerer has a source of magic somewhere in her heritage that grants her spells, bonus feats, an additional class skill, and other special abilities. This source can represent a blood relation or an extreme event involving a creature somewhere in the family's past. At 1st level, sorcerers must select a bloodline.

classFeatures.name: Eschew Materials
classFeatures.description: A sorcerer gains Eschew Materials as a bonus feat at 1st level.

coreClasses.name: Wizard
coreClasses.description: A potent spellcaster schooled in the arcane arts.

classFeatures.name: Arcane Bond
classFeatures.description: At 1st level, wizards form a powerful bond with an object or a creature. This bond can take one of two forms: a familiar or a bonded object. Once a wizard makes this choice, it is permanent and cannot be changed. A bonded object can be used once per day to cast any one spell that the wizard has in his spellbook and is capable of casting, even if the spell is not prepared.

classFeatures.name: Arcane School
classFeatures.description: A wizard can choose to specialize in one school of magic, gaining additional spells and powers based on that school. This choice must be made at 1st level, and once made, it cannot be changed. A wizard that does not select a school receives the universalist school instead. Specialist wizards receive an additional spell slot of each spell level he can cast.

classFeatures.name: Scribe Scroll
classFeatures.description: At 1st level, a wizard gains Scribe Scroll as a bonus feat.

coreClasses.weaponProficiencies: Club, dagger, heavy crossbow, light crossbow, quarterstaff
